summary: Death cinematic remains implemented, and the first level intro cinematic is now implemented as an intro-cinematic phase with Commander Kade hologram portrait, captions, ElevenLabs VO, skip handoff, cinematic camera shots over the live level, and an exit rift objective marker.
observations: No camera modes, viewmodel framing, or cutscene system exists yet.
Weapon muzzle, tracer, and wall-impact positions are derived from src/game/weapons/weaponViewPose.ts.
Pitched shot tracer distance is corrected by src/game/weapons/weaponShotMath.ts because level wall raycasts operate in flat X/Z space.
No deterministic pitch-up or pitch-down debug camera poses exist yet.
Holding fire blends camera FOV from 70 to 62 degrees and blends each weapon from its hip pose toward a centered aim pose.
snapshot.weapon.aimBlend and snapshot.weapon.cameraFovDegrees expose the current aim camera state.
Fire-button drag can adjust yaw/pitch at reduced sensitivity while the same pointer is held for fire.
Death cinematic now uses the player.hero.gadget-gremlin.apose.animated GLB and its out-of-hp clip in DeathCinematicStage.
Gameplay HUD and touch controls are hidden while phase is death-cinematic.
A qaCapture-gated debug damage hook lets Playwright force death without waiting for enemy projectiles.
The debug HUD exposes a KO button for manually triggering the death cinematic during gameplay.
DeathCinematicStage now renders in the live level scene at the player death coordinates.
The player GLB death display needs a 0.01 skinned animation scale correction because static mesh bounds and skinned animation authoring units differ by about 100x.
First level intro planning report lives at docs/cinematics/first-level-intro-cinematic-plan.md.
The recommended MVP intro uses a flat Commander Kade hologram portrait with live captions and build-time ElevenLabs VO.
Existing 3D Glyph should appear in short response shots using current idle or gun-hold animation with no mouth sync requirement.
Commander Kade is the working guide identity: calm, tactical, angular, and visually distinct from Glyph.
The first level should be framed as Rift Deck 01, a tutorial breach that teaches move, look, fire, reload HUD, 25 HP fragility, contact damage, projectiles, and exit beacon.
IntroCinematicStage now runs five timed camera shots over the existing level scene before gameplay.
The intro uses a flat Commander Kade hologram portrait and live captions for the MVP commander presentation.
Skip and natural intro completion share the same handoff: stop voice, close intro, reset combat state, then enter gameplay.
Level streaming follows the intro cinematic look target so distant shots can display without loading the whole level.
decisions: Input state must be restored after cutscenes.
Camera shake and FOV shifts must remain readable on mobile.
Gameplay camera changes must preserve or explicitly suspend weapon shot-effect pose alignment.
Future aim/FOV/recoil camera changes must preserve the flat-raycast to camera-space shot-distance conversion.
Treat hold-fire as the current aim camera mode until a formal camera state machine exists.
Any future camera mode must restore base FOV when held fire, reload, or cutscene state ends.
Death must be a distinct phase from gameplay, not only an overlay over the FPS camera.
The death cinematic should render in the live level scene so the player appears where HP reached zero.
Try Again is the primary death action and should reveal after the death read, currently after 3.2 seconds.
Return to Title and Try Again must both clear combat state before leaving death-cinematic.
The death character clone may render above nearby level geometry during the cinematic to protect readability in tight corners.
Defer a 3D Meshy commander until the flat portrait intro proves pacing, script, and mobile camera readability.
Intro cinematic should be its own intro-cinematic phase with skip available after 2 seconds and input restored before gameplay.
The first commander implementation is a flat hologram portrait, not a 3D Meshy commander model.
The first intro is now implemented as phase intro-cinematic between title and gameplay.
Intro skip unlocks after 2 seconds and must restore normal first-person gameplay state.
caughtIssues: Pointer-drag screenshot QA can approximate pitch-up/down firing, but deterministic debug look poses are needed for stable visual regression.
Held-fire centering is debug-state tested but not screenshot-regressed for each weapon silhouette.
The first standalone stage did not preserve the exact room where the player died.
Initial live-level screenshots showed only walls because the camera rotated into a blocked corner angle before the character read.
The out-of-HP animation uses skinned authoring units that made the character effectively huge until the death clone applied a 0.01 scale correction.
risks: Camera and viewmodel decisions can break touch controls and HUD readability if reviewed late.
Future strict wall occlusion for death cameras needs room-aware obstruction checks first.
Intro camera paths can clip into the current roof/walls unless authored from safe first-level coordinates.

qaRequirements: intro voice plays after user gesture
gameplay starts at normal first-person spawn
